name: mettle-syntax
description: Full language support for Mettle (.mettle): source-level debugging (breakpoints, stepping, live variable editing), go to definition, references, rename, outline, completion, signature help, compiler-backed diagnostics, run/build, and an interactive optimization report panel with one-click compiler-verified fixes.

stopOnEntry.description: Pause at the first line of the program.

cwd.description: Working directory for the program (defaults to the program's directory).

compilerArgs.description: Extra compiler arguments for the debug build (for example --native-heap).

console.description: Where the program runs: internalConsole captures output in the Debug Console; integratedTerminal runs it in a terminal so interactive stdin works.

mettle.linter.compilerEnabled.description: Run the Mettle compiler on save for semantic diagnostics.
mettle.linter.compilerEnabled.markdownDescription:
When enabled, the extension runs the `mettle` compiler on open/save and shows compiler errors and warnings in the Problems panel. Fast syntax checks still run while you type.

Requires a `mettle` binary from workspace `bin/`, a configured path, or `PATH`.

mettle.linter.compilerPath.description: Path to the mettle executable. Empty: auto-detect workspace bin/, then PATH.
mettle.linter.compilerPath.markdownDescription:
Path to the Mettle executable. Can be relative to the workspace folder or absolute.

If empty, the extension looks for `bin/mettle` or `bin/mettle.exe` under the workspace and parent folders, then uses `mettle` / `mettle.exe` on `PATH`.

mettle.linter.stdlibPath.description: Optional --stdlib directory override for the linter invocation.
mettle.linter.stdlibPath.markdownDescription:
If set, passed to the compiler as `--stdlib <dir>` when linting. Use when you maintain a custom standard library layout.

Leave empty to use the compiler's default bundled stdlib resolution.

mettle.linter.extraIncludePaths.description: Additional include directories passed as -I when compiler diagnostics run.
mettle.linter.extraIncludePaths.markdownDescription: Additional include directories passed as `-I <dir>` when compiler diagnostics run. Relative paths resolve from the workspace folder.

mettle.linter.compilerTimeoutMs.description: Maximum time in milliseconds to wait for one compiler diagnostics run.

mettle.explain.inlineHints.description: While the optimization report panel is open, annotate loop lines in the editor with their vectorization outcome.
mettle.explain.inlineHints.markdownDescription: While the optimization report panel is open, loop lines in the source editor get a subtle end-of-line annotation: `vectorized -> vfmadd231ps ...` or the refusal reason. Hover for the full reason/fix/verified detail.

mettle.explain.refreshOnSave.description: Recompile and refresh the optimization report panel when its source file is saved.

mettle.explain.timeoutMs.description: Maximum time in milliseconds for one --release --explain compile (verified fix simulations included).

mettle.codegen.asmSyntax.description: Default assembly syntax for the Codegen Annotations panel (toggle live in the panel).

mettle.codegen.refreshOnSave.description: Recompile and refresh the Codegen Annotations panel when its source file is saved.

mettle.codegen.timeoutMs.description: Maximum time in milliseconds for one --annotate-asm compile.

mettle.hints.parameterNames.description: Show parameter-name inlay hints at call sites.
mettle.hints.parameterNames.markdownDescription:
Show parameter-name inlay hints at call sites of functions the extension can resolve. `off` (default) shows none; `literals` hints only literal arguments like `dot(a, b, 64)`; `all` hints every argument.

Note: VS Code renders inlay hints in the minimap at an unscaled size, so enabling this paints oversized hint text into the minimap. Set `editor.minimap.renderCharacters` to `false` if that bothers you.

mettle.codeLens.enabled.description: Show Run/Build/Optimization Report CodeLens above main, and per-function loop statistics while the report panel is open.

mettle.run.release.description: Pass --release when building via Mettle: Run File / Build File.

mettle.run.extraArgs.description: Extra compiler arguments for Mettle: Run File / Build File (for example --native-heap).

mettle.profile.runTimeoutMs.description: Maximum time (ms) the instrumented binary may run during Mettle: Profile Codegen before it is killed.
